title: Send As Distribution Group

posts.author: Brad98 (Bradley Lomax)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I think it would be a very used and useful feature for the Outlook Send Email automation be able to use a Distribution Group as the Send As email address, not just emails with log in credentials.

What are you trying to achieve

Distribution group email addresses used as Send As addresses where as its just emails with log in credentials at the moment

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Brad98, Welcome to the community!

This would definitely be a great addition!

As a workaround, we typically use a service account set up as a standard user in our directory, along with a separate monday.com account. This allows us to connect it to the Outlook integration and send emails from that service address.

It’s not the same as using a distribution group directly, but it helps achieve a similar outcome in the meantime.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I get what you’re trying to achieve here, but using a distribution group as a “send as” address isn’t really how those are meant to work.

Distribution groups are typically for receiving and distributing emails, not acting as a true sender identity. What you’re likely looking for is the ability to send from a shared/team address like support@ or hr@ without tying it to a specific user.

There are a couple ways to handle this today depending on your setup and how IT manages your accounts:

  • service account or “bot” user with send permissions

  • Microsoft 365 shared mailbox (now supported in monday Service)
